Annual Exam - 2025

Code: 65/1/2 (set-2)

  1. Please check that this question paper contains 23 printed pages.
  2. Please check that this question paper contains 38 questions.
  3. Q.P. Code given on the right hand side of the question paper should be written on the title page of the answer-book by the candidate.
  4. Please write down the serial number of the question in the answer-book at the given place before attempting it.
  5. 15 minute time has been allotted to read this question paper. The question paper will be distributed at 10.15 a.m. From 10.15 a.m. to 10.30 a.m., the candidates will read the question paper only and will not write any answer on the answer book during this period.

MATHEMATICS

Time allowed:3 hours
Maximum Marks:80

General Instructions :

Read the following instructions very carefully and strictly follow them:

  1. This Question paper contains 38 questions. All questions are compulsory.
  2. Question paper is divided into FIVE Sections - Section A, B, C, D and E.
  3. In Section A - Question Number 1 to 18 are Multiple Choice Questions (MCQs) and Question Number {tex}19 \& 20{/tex} are Assertion-Reason based questions of 1 mark each.
  4. In Section B - Question Number 21 to 25 are Very Short Answer (VSA) type questions, carrying 2 marks each.
  5. In Section C - Question Number 26 to 31 are Short Answer (SA) type questions, carrying 3 marks each.
  6. In Section D - Question Number 32 to 35 are Long Answer (LA) type questions, carrying 5 marks each.
  7. In Section E - Question Number 36 to 38 are case study based questions, carrying 4 marks each.
  8. There is no overall choice. However, an internal choice has been provided in 2 questions in Section - B, 3 questions in Section - C, 2 questions in Section - {tex}\boldsymbol{D}{/tex} and {tex}{2}{/tex} questions in Section - {tex}\boldsymbol{E}{/tex}.
  9. Use of calculator is NOT allowed.

Section - A

This section comprises of {tex}{2 0}{/tex} Multiple Choice Questions (MCQs) of {tex}{1}{/tex} mark each. ({tex} 20 \times 1=20 {/tex})
1.

If E and F are two independent events such that {tex}{P}({E})=\frac{2}{3}, {P}({F})=\frac{3}{7}{/tex}, then {tex}{P}({E} / \overline{{F}}){/tex} is equal to:

a. {tex}\frac{1}{6}{/tex}
b. {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex}
c. {tex}\frac{2}{3}{/tex}
d. {tex}\frac{7}{9}{/tex}
1
2.

If {tex}\vec{\alpha}=\hat{i}-4 \hat{j}+9 \hat{k}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{\beta}=2 \hat{i}-8 \hat{j}+\lambda \hat{k}{/tex} are two mutually parallel vectors, then {tex}\lambda{/tex} is equal to:

a. -18
b. 18
c. {tex}\frac{-34}{9}{/tex}
d. {tex}\frac{34}{9}{/tex}
1
3.

{tex}\int \frac{1-2 \sin x}{\cos ^{2} x} {~d} x{/tex} is equal to:

a. {tex}\tan x-2 \sec x+{C}{/tex}
b. {tex}-\tan x+2 \sec x+{C}{/tex}
c. {tex}-\tan x-2 \sec x+{C}{/tex}
d. {tex}\tan x+2 \sec x+{C}{/tex}
1
4.

If {tex}\vec{a}+\vec{b}+\vec{c}=\vec{0},|\vec{a}|=\sqrt{37},|\vec{b}|=3{/tex} and {tex}|\vec{c}|=4{/tex}, then angle between {tex}\vec{b}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{c}{/tex} is

a. {tex}\frac{\pi}{6}{/tex}
b. {tex}\frac{\pi}{4}{/tex}
c. {tex}\frac{\pi}{3}{/tex}
d. {tex}\frac{\pi}{2}{/tex}
1
5.

The graph of a trigonometric function is as shown. Which of the following will represent graph of its inverse?

a.
b.
c.
d.
1
6.

If A is a square matrix of order 3 such that {tex}\operatorname{det}({A})=9{/tex}, then {tex}\operatorname{det}\left(9 {~A}^{-1}\right){/tex} is equal to

a. 9
b. {tex}9^{2}{/tex}
c. {tex}9^{3}{/tex}
d. {tex}9^{4}{/tex}
1
7.

If {tex}{f}(x)=|x|+|x-1|{/tex}, then which of the following is correct?

a. {tex}{f}(x){/tex} is both continuous and differentiable, at {tex}x=0{/tex} and {tex}x=1{/tex}.
b. {tex}{f}(x){/tex} is differentiable but not continuous, at {tex}x=0{/tex} and {tex}x=1{/tex}.
c. {tex}{f}(x){/tex} is continuous but not differentiable, at {tex}x=0{/tex} and {tex}x=1{/tex}.
d. {tex}{f}(x){/tex} is neither continuous nor differentiable, at {tex}x=0{/tex} and {tex}x=1{/tex}.
1
8.

Which of the following is not a homogeneous function of {tex}x{/tex} and y?

a. {tex}y^{2}-x y{/tex}
b. {tex}x-3 y{/tex}
c. {tex}\sin ^{2} \frac{{y}}{x}+\frac{{y}}{x}{/tex}
d. {tex}\tan x-\sec y{/tex}
1
9.

Let A be a matrix of order {tex}{m} \times {n}{/tex} and B is a matrix such that {tex}{A}^{{T}} {B}{/tex} and {tex}{BA}^{{T}}{/tex} are defined. Then, the order of {tex}B{/tex} is:

a. {tex}{m} \times {m}{/tex}
b. {tex}n \times n{/tex}
c. {tex}m \times n{/tex}
d. {tex}n \times m{/tex}
1
10.

If the feasible region of a linear programming problem with objective function {tex}{Z}={a} x+{/tex} by, is bounded, then which of the following is correct?

a. It will only have a maximum value.
b. It will only have a minimum value.
c. It will have both maximum and minimum values.
d. It will have neither maximum nor minimum value.
1
11.

If {tex}{A}=\left[\begin{array}{ccc}-1 & 0 & 0 \\ 0 & 1 & 0 \\ 0 & 0 & 1\end{array}\right]{/tex}, then {tex}{A}^{-1}{/tex} is

a. {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc}-1 & 0 & 0 \\ 0 & -1 & 0 \\ 0 & 0 & -1\end{array}\right]{/tex}
b. {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc}1 & 0 & 0 \\ 0 & -1 & 0 \\ 0 & 0 & -1\end{array}\right]{/tex}
c. {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc}-1 & 0 & 0 \\ 0 & -1 & 0 \\ 0 & 0 & 1\end{array}\right]{/tex}
d. {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc}-1 & 0 & 0 \\ 0 & 1 & 0 \\ 0 & 0 & 1\end{array}\right]{/tex}
1
12.

The integrating factor of the differential equation {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}+y=\frac{1+y}{x}{/tex} is:

a. {tex}\frac{x}{e^{x}}{/tex}
b. {tex}\frac{e^{x}}{x}{/tex}
c. xex
d. ex
1
13.

Let {tex}{A}=\left[{a}_{{ij}}\right]{/tex} be a square matrix of order 3 such that {tex}{a}_{{ij}}=\hat{{j}}-2 \hat{{i}}{/tex}. Then which of the following is true?

a. {tex}{a}_{12} \gt 0{/tex}
b. all {tex}{a}_{{ij}} \lt 0{/tex}
c. {tex}{a}_{13}+{a}_{31}=-6{/tex}
d. {tex}{a}_{23} \gt {a}_{32}{/tex}
1
14.

The absolute maximum value of function {tex}{f}(x)=x^{3}-3 x+2{/tex} in {tex}[0,2]{/tex} is:

a. 0
b. 2
c. 4
d. 5
1
15.

If {tex} \int \frac{2^{1 / x}}{x^{2}} d x=k 2^{1 / x}+C,{/tex} then k is equal to

a. {tex}-\frac{1}{\log _{e} 2}{/tex}
b. {tex}-\log _{e} 2{/tex}
c. {tex}-1{/tex}
d. {tex}\frac 12{/tex}
1
16.

If A and B are invertible matrices, then which of the following is not correct?

a. adj. A = |A|. A–1
b. det(A)–1 = [det (A)]–1
c. (AB)–1 = B–1 A–1
d. (A + B)–1 = B–1 + A–1
1
17.

The corner points of the feasible region in the graphical representation of a linear programming problem are (2, 72), (15, 20) and (40, 15). If z = 18x + 9y be the objective function, then:

a. z is maximum at (2, 72), minimum at (15, 20)
b. z is maximum at (15, 20), minimum at (40, 15)
c. z is maximum at (40, 15), minimum at (15, 20)
d. z is maximum at (40, 15), minimum at (2, 72)
1
18.

The area of the shaded region bounded by the curves {tex}{y}^{2}=x, x=4{/tex} and the {tex}x{/tex}-axis is given by

a. {tex}\int_{0}^{4} x {~d} x{/tex}
b. {tex}\int_{0}^{2} {y}^{2} {dy}{/tex}
c. {tex}2 \int_{0}^{4} \sqrt{x} {~d} x{/tex}
d. {tex}\int_{0}^{4} \sqrt{x} {~d} x{/tex}
1

Assertion - Reason Based Questions

Direction: Question numbers 19 and 20 are Assertion (A) and Reason (R) based questions carrying 1 mark each. Two statements are given, one labelled Assertion (A) and other labelled Reason (R). Select the correct answer from the options (A), (B), (C) and (D) as given below.

  1. Both Assertion (A) and Reason (R) are true and the Reason (R) is the correct explanation of the Assertion (A).
  2. Both Assertion (A) and Reason (R) are true, but Reason (R) is not the correct explanation of the Assertion (A).
  3. Assertion (A) is true, but Reason (R) is false.
  4. Assertion (A) is false, but Reason (R) is true.
19.

Assertion (A): Let Z be the set of integers. A function {tex}{f}: {Z} \rightarrow {Z}{/tex} defined as {tex}{f}(x)=3 x-5, \forall x \in {Z}{/tex} is a bijective.
Reason (R): A function is a bijective if it is both surjective and injective.

a. Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.
b. Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A.
c. A is true but R is false.
d. A is false but R is true.
1
20.

Assertion (A): {tex}{f}(x)=\left\{\begin{array}{cc}3 x-8, & x \leq 5 \\ 2 {k}, & x \gt 5\end{array}\right.{/tex} {tex}\text { is continuous at } x=5 \text { for } {k}=\frac{5}{2} \text {. }{/tex}
Reason (R): For a function f to be continuous at {tex}x={a}{/tex}, {tex}\lim \limits_{x \rightarrow {a}^{-}} {f}(x)=\lim \limits_{x \rightarrow {a}^{+}} {f}(x)={f}({a}){/tex}

a. Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.
b. Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A.
c. A is true but R is false.
d. A is false but R is true.
1

Section - B

This section comprises of 5 very short answer (VSA) type questions of 2 marks each. (5{tex}\times{/tex}2 = 10)
21.(i)

Two friends while flying kites from different locations, find the strings of their kites crossing each other. The strings can be represented by vectors {tex}\vec{a}=3 \hat{i}+\hat{j}+2 \hat{k}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{b}=2 \hat{i}-2 \hat{j}+4 \hat{k}{/tex}. Determine the angle formed between the kite strings. Assume there is no slack in the strings.

 2

OR
(ii)

Find a vector of magnitude 21 units in the direction opposite to that of {tex}\overrightarrow{A B}{/tex} where {tex}A{/tex} and {tex}B{/tex} are the points {tex}A(2,1,3){/tex} and {tex}B(8,-1,0){/tex} respectively.

 2
22.

Find the values of 'a' for which {tex}{f}(x)=x^{2}-2 {a} x+{b}{/tex} is an increasing function for {tex}x \gt 0{/tex}.

 2
23.(i)

Differentiate {tex}2^{\cos ^{2} x}{/tex} w.r.t {tex}\cos ^{2} x{/tex}.

 2

OR
(ii)

If {tex}\tan ^{-1}\left(x^{2}+y^{2}\right)=a^{2}{/tex}, then find {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}{/tex}.

 2
24.

Evaluate: {tex}\sin ^{-1}\left(\sin \frac{3 \pi}{5}\right){/tex}

 2
25.

The diagonals of a parallelogram are given by {tex}\vec{a}=2 \hat{i}-\hat{j}+\hat{k}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{{b}}=\hat{{i}}+3 \hat{{j}}-\hat{{k}}{/tex}. Find the area of the parallelogram.

 2

Section - C

This section comprises of 6 short answer (SA) type questions of 3 marks each. (6{tex}\times{/tex}3 = 18)
26.(i)

Verify that lines given by {tex}\vec{r}=(1-\lambda) \hat{i}+(\lambda-2) \hat{j}+(3-2 \lambda) \hat{k}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{r}=(\mu+1) \hat{i}+(2 \mu-1) \hat{j}-(2 \mu+1) \hat{k}{/tex} are skew lines. Hence, find shortest distance between the lines.

 3

OR
(ii)

During a cricket match, the position of the bowler, the wicket keeper and the leg slip fielder are in a line given by {tex}\vec{B}=2 \hat{i}+8 \hat{j}{/tex}, {tex}\vec{{W}}=6 \hat{{i}}+12 \hat{{j}}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{{F}}=12 \hat{{i}}+18 \hat{{j}}{/tex} respectively. Calculate the ratio in which the wicketkeeper divides the line segment joining the bowler and the leg slip fielder.

 3
27.

Solve the following linear programming problem graphically:
Maximise {tex}Z=20 x+30 y{/tex}
Subject to the constraints:
{tex}x+y \leq 80{/tex}
{tex}2 x+3 y \geq 100{/tex}
{tex}x \geq 14{/tex}
{tex}y \geq 14{/tex}

 3
28.

The area of an expanding rectangle is increasing at the rate of {tex}48 {cm}^{2} / {s}{/tex}. The length of the rectangle is always square of its breadth. At what rate the length of rectangle increasing at an instant, when breadth {tex}=4.5 {~cm}{/tex}?

 3
29.(i)

The probability distribution for the number of students being absent in a class on a Saturday is as follows:

{tex}{X}{/tex} 0 2 4 5
{tex}{P}({X}){/tex} p 2 p 3 p p

Where X is the number of students absent.

  1. Calculate p. (1)
  2. Calculate the mean of the number of absent students on Saturday. (2)
 3

OR
(ii)

For the vacancy advertised in the newspaper, 3000 candidates submitted their applications. From the data it was revealed that two third of the total applicants were females and other were males. The selection for the job was done through a written test. The performance of the applicants indicates that the probability of a male getting a distinction in written test is 0.4 and that a female getting a distinction is 0.35. Find the probability that the candidate chosen at random will have a distinction in the written test.

 3
30.(i)

Find: {tex}\int \frac{\cos 2 x}{(\sin x+\cos x)^{2}} d x{/tex}

 3

OR
(ii)

Evaluate: {tex}\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \frac{5 \sin x+3 \cos x}{\sin x+\cos x} {~d} x{/tex}

 3
31.

Sketch the graph of {tex}{y}=|x+3|{/tex} and find the area of the region enclosed by the curve, {tex}x{/tex}-axis, between {tex}x=-6{/tex} and {tex}x=0{/tex}, using integration.

 3

Section - D

This section comprises of {tex}{4}{/tex} Long Answer (LA) type questions of {tex}{5}{/tex} marks each. 4 {tex}\times{/tex} 5 = 20
32.(i)

Differentiate {tex}\tan ^{-1} \frac{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}}{x}{/tex} w.r.t. {tex}\cos ^{-1}\left(2 x \sqrt{1-x^{2}}\right), x \in\left(\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}, 1\right){/tex}

 4

OR
(ii)

Find {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dx}},{/tex} if {tex}y = {x^{\tan x}} + \sqrt {\frac{{{x^2} + 1}}{2}} {/tex}

 5
35.

Find the absolute maximum and minimum values of a function f given by
f (x) = 2x3 – 15x2 + 36x +1 on the interval [1, 5].

 5
34.

A school wants to allocate students into three clubs : Sports, Music and Drama, under following conditions:

  • The number of students in Sports club should be equal to the sum of the number of students in Music and Drama club.
  • The number of students in Music club should be 20 more than half the number of students in Sports club.
  • The total number of students to be allocated in all three clubs are 180.

Find the number of students allocated to different clubs, using matrix method.

 5
35.(i)

Find the image {tex}{A}^{\prime}{/tex} of the point {tex}{A}(1,6,3){/tex} in the line {tex}\frac{x}{1}=\frac{{y}-1}{2}=\frac{{z}-2}{3}{/tex}. Also, find the equation of the line joining A and {tex}{A}^{\prime}{/tex}.

 5

OR
(ii)

Find a point P on the line {tex}\frac{x+5}{1}=\frac{{y}+3}{4}=\frac{{z}-6}{-9}{/tex} such that its distance from point {tex}{Q}(2,4,-1){/tex} is 7 units. Also, find the equation of line joining P and Q.

 5

Section - E

This section comprises of 3 case study/passage based questions of 4 marks each. {tex} 3 \times 4=12 {/tex}

Case Study - 1
36.


A bank offers loan to its customers on different types of interest namely, fixed rate, floating rate and variable rate. From the past data with the bank, it is known that a customer avails loan on fixed rate, floating rate or variable rate with probabilities {tex}10 \%, 20 \%{/tex} and {tex}70 \%{/tex} respectively. A customer after availing loan can pay the loan or default on loan repayment. The bank data suggests that the probability that a person defaults on loan after availing it at fixed rate, floating rate and variable rate is {tex}5 \%, 3 \%{/tex} and {tex}1 \%{/tex} respectively.
Based on the above information, answer the following :

  1. What is the probability that a customer after availing the loan will default on the loan repayment? (2)
  2. A customer after availing the loan, defaults on loan repayment. What is the probability that he availed the loan at a variable rate of interest? (2)
 4

Case Study - 2
37.


A technical company is designing a rectangular solar panel installation on a roof using 300 metres of boundary material. The design includes a partition running parallel to one of the sides dividing the area (roof) into two sections.
Let the length of the side perpendicular to the partition be {tex}x{/tex} metres and with parallel to the partition be {tex}y{/tex} metres.
Based on this information, answer the following questions:

  1. Write the equation for the total boundary material used in the boundary and parallel to the partition in terms of {tex}x{/tex} and {tex}y{/tex}. (1)
  2. Write the area of the solar panel as a function of {tex}x{/tex}. (1)
    1. Find the critical points of the area function. Use second derivative test to determine critical points at the maximum area. Also, find the maximum area. (2)

      OR

    2. Using first derivative test, calculate the maximum area the company can enclose with the 300 metres of boundary material, considering the parallel partition. (2)
 4

Case Study - 3
38.

A class-room teacher is keen to assess the learning of her students the concept of "relations" taught to them. She writes the following five relations each defined on the set {tex}{A}=\{1,2,3\}{/tex}:
{tex}{R}_{1}=\{(2,3),(3,2)\}{/tex}
{tex}{R}_{2}=\{(1,2),(1,3),(3,2)\}{/tex}
{tex}{R}_{3}=\{(1,2),(2,1),(1,1)\}{/tex}
{tex}{R}_{4}=\{(1,1),(1,2),(3,3),(2,2)\}{/tex}
{tex}{R}_{5}=\{(1,1),(1,2),(3,3),(2,2),(2,1),(2,3),(3,2)\}{/tex}
The students are asked to answer the following questions about the above relations:

  1. Identify the relation which is reflexive, transitive but not symmetric. (1)
  2. Identify the relation which is reflexive and symmetric but not transitive. (1)
    1. Identify the relations which are symmetric but neither reflexive nor transitive. (2)

      OR

    2. What pairs should be added to the relation {tex}R_{2}{/tex} to make it an equivalence relation? (2)
 4

The integrating factor of the differential equation {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}+y=\frac{1+y}{x}{/tex} is:

a. {tex}\frac{x}{e^{x}}{/tex}
b. {tex}\frac{e^{x}}{x}{/tex}
c. xex
d. ex
Correct Answer: (b) {tex}\frac{e^{x}}{x}{/tex}
Explanation:

We have, {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}+y=\frac{1+y}{x}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{1}{x}+\frac{y(1-x)}{x}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d y}{d x}-\left(\frac{1-x}{x}\right) y=\frac{1}{x}{/tex} 
This is a linear differential equation.
On comparing it with {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}+P y=Q{/tex}, we get
{tex}P=\frac{-(1-x)}{x}, Q=\frac{1}{x}{/tex} 
I.F. = {tex}e^{\int P d x}=e^{-\int \frac{1-x}{x} d x}{/tex} 
{tex}=e^{x-\log x}{/tex} or {tex}\frac{e^{x}}{x}{/tex}

b. {tex}-\log _{e} 2{/tex}
c. {tex}-1{/tex}
d. {tex}\frac 12{/tex}
Correct Answer: (a) {tex}-\frac{1}{\log _{e} 2}{/tex}
Explanation:

We have to find:{tex}I=\int \frac{2^{\frac{1}{x}}}{x^{2}} d x{/tex}
{tex}\text { Put } \frac{1}{x}=t{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{-1}{x^{2}} d x=d t \Rightarrow \frac{1}{x^{2}} d x=-d t{/tex}
{tex}\therefore ~I=\int 2^{t}(-d t){/tex}
{tex}I=\frac{-2^{t}}{\log _{e} 2}+c{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow I=\frac{-2^{\frac{1}{x}}}{\log _{e} 2}+c{/tex}

a. adj. A = |A|. A–1
b. det(A)–1 = [det (A)]–1
c. (AB)–1 = B–1 A–1
d. (A + B)–1 = B–1 + A–1
Correct Answer: (d) (A + B)–1 = B–1 + A–1
Explanation:

Given A and B are invertible matrices.
Now, {tex}(A \cdot B) B^{-1} A^{-1}{/tex} = A(BB-1) A-1 = AIA-1 = (AI)A-1 = AA-1 = I
{tex}\Rightarrow (A B)^{-1}=B^{-1} A^{-1}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} A A-1 = I
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} |A A-1| = |I|
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} |A||A-1| = 1
{tex}\Rightarrow \left|A^{-1}\right|=\frac{1}{|A|}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \operatorname{det}(A)^{-1}=[\operatorname{det}(A)]^{-1}{/tex}
Also we know that {tex}A^{-1}=\frac{adj \cdot A}{|A|}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \text { adj. } A=|A| \cdot A^{-1}{/tex}
{tex}(A+B)^{-1}=\frac{1}{|A+B|} \operatorname{adj} .(A+B){/tex}
But {tex}B^{-1}+A^{-1}=\frac{1}{|B|} \text { adj. } B+\frac{1}{|A|} \operatorname{adj} . A{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow (A+B)^{-1} \neq B^{-1}+A^{-1}{/tex}

Find the absolute maximum and minimum values of a function f given by
f (x) = 2x3 – 15x2 + 36x +1 on the interval [1, 5].

Solution:

We have
f(x) = 2x3 – 15x2 + 36x + 1
or f ′(x) = 6x2 – 30x + 36 = 6 (x – 3) (x – 2)
Note that f ′(x) = 0 gives x = 2 and x = 3.
We shall now evaluate the value of f at these points and at the end points of the interval [1, 5], i.e., at x = 1, x = 2, x = 3 and at x = 5. So
f(1) = 2(13) – 15(12) + 36 (1) + 1 = 24
f(2) = 2(23) – 15(22) + 36 (2) + 1 = 29
f(3) = 2(33) – 15(32) + 36 (3) + 1 = 28
f(5) = 2(53) – 15(52) + 36 (5) + 1 = 56
Thus, we conclude that absolute maximum value of f on [1, 5] is 56, occurring at x =5, and absolute minimum value of f on [1, 5] is 24 which occurs at x = 1.

A school wants to allocate students into three clubs : Sports, Music and Drama, under following conditions:

  • The number of students in Sports club should be equal to the sum of the number of students in Music and Drama club.
  • The number of students in Music club should be 20 more than half the number of students in Sports club.
  • The total number of students to be allocated in all three clubs are 180.

Find the number of students allocated to different clubs, using matrix method.

Solution:

Let {tex}x, y{/tex} and {tex}z{/tex} be the no. of students allocated to Sports, Music and Drama clubs respectively.
Here, {tex}x=y+z, y=\frac{x}{2}+20, x+y+z=180{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow x-y-z=0, x-2 y=-40, x+y+z=180 {/tex}
Given equations can be written as {tex}A X=B{/tex}
where, {tex}A=\left[\begin{array}{ccc}1 & -1 & -1 \\ 1 & -2 & 0 \\ 1 & 1 & 1\end{array}\right], B=\left[\begin{array}{c}0 \\ -40 \\ 180\end{array}\right], {/tex} {tex}X=\left[\begin{array}{l}x \\ y \\ z\end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}|A|=-4 \neq 0 \Rightarrow A^{-1}{/tex} exists.
{tex} \operatorname{adj} A=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -2 & 0 & -2 \\ -1 & 2 & -1 \\ 3 & -2 & -1 \end{array}\right] {/tex}
{tex} A^{-1}=\frac{1}{|A|} \times \operatorname{adj} A=\frac{1}{4}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 2 & 0 & 2 \\ 1 & -2 & 1 \\ -3 & 2 & 1 \end{array}\right] {/tex}
{tex} X=A^{-1} B {/tex}
{tex} =\frac{1}{4}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 2 & 0 & 2 \\ 1 & -2 & 1 \\ -3 & 2 & 1 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{c} 0 \\ -40 \\ 180 \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{l} 90 \\ 65 \\ 25 \end{array}\right] {/tex}
{tex} \therefore x=90, y=65, z=25 {/tex}
Number of students allocated in sports, music and drama are 90,65 and 25 respectively.

The bank data suggests that the probability that a person defaults on loan after availing it at fixed rate, floating rate and variable rate is {tex}5 \%, 3 \%{/tex} and {tex}1 \%{/tex} respectively.
Based on the above information, answer the following :

  1. What is the probability that a customer after availing the loan will default on the loan repayment? (2)
  2. A customer after availing the loan, defaults on loan repayment. What is the probability that he availed the loan at a variable rate of interest? (2)
Solution:

{tex}E_1{/tex} : customer avails loan on fixed rate
{tex}E_2{/tex} : customer avails loan on floating rate
{tex}E_3{/tex} : customer avails loan on variable rate
A:the person defaults on the loan
{tex} {P}\left(E_1\right)=\frac{1}{10}, {P}\left(E_2\right)=\frac{2}{10}, {P}\left(E_3\right)=\frac{7}{10} {/tex}
{tex} {P}\left(A \mid E_1\right) =\frac{5}{100}, {P}\left(A \mid E_2\right)=\frac{3}{100}, {P}\left(A \mid E_3\right)=\frac{1}{100} {/tex}

  1. {tex}{P}(A) ={P}\left(E_1\right) \cdot {P}\left(A \mid E_1\right)+{P}\left(E_2\right) {/tex}{tex}\cdot {P}\left(A \mid E_2\right)+{P}\left(E_3\right) \cdot {P}\left(A \mid E_3\right) {/tex}
    {tex} =\frac{1}{10} \times \frac{5}{100}+\frac{2}{10} \times \frac{3}{100}+\frac{7}{10} \times \frac{1}{100} {/tex}
    {tex} =\frac{18}{1000} \text { or } \frac{9}{500} {/tex}
  2. {tex} {P}\left(E_3 \mid A\right) {/tex} {tex}=\frac{{P}\left(E_3\right) \cdot {P}\left(A \mid E_3\right)}{{P}\left(E_1\right) \cdot {P}\left(A \mid E_1\right)+{P}\left(E_2\right) \cdot {P}\left(A \mid E_2\right)+{P}\left(E_3\right) \cdot {P}\left(A \mid E_3\right)} {/tex}
    {tex} =\frac{\frac{7}{10} \times \frac{1}{100}}{\frac{18}{1000}} {/tex}
    {tex} =\frac{7}{18} {/tex}

A technical company is designing a rectangular solar panel installation on a roof using 300 metres of boundary material. The design includes a partition running parallel to one of the sides dividing the area (roof) into two sections.

  2. Write the area of the solar panel as a function of {tex}x{/tex}. (1)
    1. Find the critical points of the area function. Use second derivative test to determine critical points at the maximum area. Also, find the maximum area. (2)

      OR

    2. Using first derivative test, calculate the maximum area the company can enclose with the 300 metres of boundary material, considering the parallel partition. (2)
Solution:
  1. {tex}{2} \boldsymbol{x}+{3} \boldsymbol{y}={3 0 0}{/tex}
  2. {tex}A=x y=\frac{x}{3}(300-2 x){/tex}
    1. {tex}A=\frac{x}{3}(300-2 x)=\frac{1}{3}\left(300 x-2 x^{2}\right){/tex}
      {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d A}{d x}=\frac{1}{3}(300-4 x){/tex}
      For critical points, put {tex}\frac{d A}{d x}=0 \Rightarrow x=75{/tex}
      Also, {tex}\frac{d^{2} A}{d x^{2}}=-\frac{4}{3} \lt 0{/tex}. So, {tex}A{/tex} is maximum at {tex}x=75{/tex}

      {tex}x=75{/tex} from left to right, which means {tex}x=75{/tex} is the point of maximum.
      Also, maximum area is {tex}A=\frac{{7 5}}{{3}}({3 0 0}-{1 5 0})={3 7 5 0} {m}^{{2}}{/tex}

Correct Answer: (c) It will have both maximum and minimum values. Explanation: It will have both maximum and minimum values. 1 11. If {tex}{A}=\left[\begin{array}{ccc}-1 & 0 & 0 \\ 0 & 1 & 0 \\ 0 & 0 & 1\end{array}\right]{/tex} , then {tex}{A}^{-1}{/tex} is a. {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc}-1 & 0 & 0 \\ 0 & -1 & 0 \\ 0 & 0 & -1\end{array}\right]{/tex} b. {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc}1 & 0 & 0 \\ 0 & -1 & 0 \\ 0 & 0 & -1\end{array}\right]{/tex} c. {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc}-1 & 0 & 0 \\ 0 & -1 & 0 \\ 0 & 0 & 1\end{array}\right]{/tex} d. {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc}-1 & 0 & 0 \\ 0 & 1 & 0 \\ 0 & 0 & 1\end{array}\right]{/tex} Correct Answer: (d) {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc}-1 & 0 & 0 \\ 0 & 1 & 0 \\ 0 & 0 & 1\end{array}\right]{/tex} Explanation: {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc}-1 & 0 & 0 \\ 0 & 1 & 0 \\ 0 & 0 & 1\end{array}\right]{/tex} 1 12. The integrating factor of the differential equation {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}+y=\frac{1+y}{x}{/tex} is: a. {tex}\frac{x}{e^{x}}{/tex} b. {tex}\frac{e^{x}}{x}{/tex} c. xex d. ex Correct Answer: (b) {tex}\frac{e^{x}}{x}{/tex} Explanation: We have, {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}+y=\frac{1+y}{x}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{1}{x}+\frac{y(1-x)}{x}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d y}{d x}-\left(\frac{1-x}{x}\right) y=\frac{1}{x}{/tex} This is a linear differential equation. On comparing it with {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}+P y=Q{/tex} , we get {tex}P=\frac{-(1-x)}{x}, Q=\frac{1}{x}{/tex} I.F. = {tex}e^{\int P d x}=e^{-\int \frac{1-x}{x} d x}{/tex} {tex}=e^{x-\log x}{/tex} or {tex}\frac{e^{x}}{x}{/tex} 1 13. Then which of the following is true? a. {tex}{a}_{12} \gt 0{/tex} b. all {tex}{a}_{{ij}} \lt 0{/tex} c. {tex}{a}_{13}+{a}_{31}=-6{/tex} d. {tex}{a}_{23} \gt {a}_{32}{/tex} Correct Answer: (d) {tex}{a}_{23} \gt {a}_{32}{/tex} Explanation: {tex}{a}_{23} \gt {a}_{32}{/tex} 1 14. The absolute maximum value of function {tex}{f}(x)=x^{3}-3 x+2{/tex} in {tex}[0,2]{/tex} is: a. 0 b. 2 c. 4 d. 5 Correct Answer: (c) 4 Explanation: 4 1 15. If {tex} \int \frac{2^{1 / x}}{x^{2}} d x=k 2^{1 / x}+C,{/tex} then k is equal to a. {tex}-\frac{1}{\log _{e} 2}{/tex} b. {tex}-\log _{e} 2{/tex} c. {tex}-1{/tex} d. {tex}\frac 12{/tex} Correct Answer: (a) {tex}-\frac{1}{\log _{e} 2}{/tex} Explanation: We have to find: {tex}I=\int \frac{2^{\frac{1}{x}}}{x^{2}} d x{/tex} {tex}\text { Put } \frac{1}{x}=t{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{-1}{x^{2}} d x=d t \Rightarrow \frac{1}{x^{2}} d x=-d t{/tex} {tex}\therefore ~I=\int 2^{t}(-d t){/tex} {tex}I=\frac{-2^{t}}{\log _{e} 2}+c{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow I=\frac{-2^{\frac{1}{x}}}{\log _{e} 2}+c{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow ~k=\frac{-1}{\log _{e} 2}{/tex} 1 16. (A + B)–1 = B–1 + A–1 Correct Answer: (d) (A + B)–1 = B–1 + A–1 Explanation: Given A and B are invertible matrices. Now, {tex}(A \cdot B) B^{-1} A^{-1}{/tex} = A(BB -1 ) A -1 = AIA -1 = (AI)A -1 = AA -1 = I {tex}\Rightarrow (A B)^{-1}=B^{-1} A^{-1}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} A A -1 = I {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} |A A -1 | = |I| {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} |A||A -1 | = 1 {tex}\Rightarrow \left|A^{-1}\right|=\frac{1}{|A|}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \operatorname{det}(A)^{-1}=[\operatorname{det}(A)]^{-1}{/tex} Also we know that {tex}A^{-1}=\frac{adj \cdot A}{|A|}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \text { adj. } A=|A| \cdot A^{-1}{/tex} {tex}(A+B)^{-1}=\frac{1}{|A+B|} \operatorname{adj} .(A+B){/tex} But {tex}B^{-1}+A^{-1}=\frac{1}{|B|} \text { adj. } B+\frac{1}{|A|} \operatorname{adj} . A{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow (A+B)^{-1} \neq B^{-1}+A^{-1}{/tex} 1 17. The area of the shaded region bounded by the curves {tex}{y}^{2}=x, x=4{/tex} and the {tex}x{/tex} -axis is given by a. {tex}\int_{0}^{4} x {~d} x{/tex} b. {tex}\int_{0}^{2} {y}^{2} {dy}{/tex} c. {tex}2 \int_{0}^{4} \sqrt{x} {~d} x{/tex} d. {tex}\int_{0}^{4} \sqrt{x} {~d} x{/tex} Correct Answer: (d) {tex}\int_{0}^{4} \sqrt{x} {~d} x{/tex} Explanation: {tex}\int_{0}^{4} \sqrt{x} {~d} x{/tex} 1 Assertion - Reason Based Questions Direction: Question numbers 19 and 20 are Assertion (A) and Reason (R) based questions carrying 1 mark each. Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A. b. Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A. c. A is true but R is false. d. A is false but R is true. (5 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 = 10) 21. (i) Two friends while flying kites from different locations, find the strings of their kites crossing each other. The strings can be represented by vectors {tex}\vec{a}=3 \hat{i}+\hat{j}+2 \hat{k}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{b}=2 \hat{i}-2 \hat{j}+4 \hat{k}{/tex} . Determine the angle formed between the kite strings. Assume there is no slack in the strings. Solution: Let the required angle between the kite strings be {tex}\theta{/tex} . {tex} \text { Then, } \cos \theta=\frac{\vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}}{|\vec{a}||\vec{b}|} {/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \cos \theta=\frac{(3 \hat{i}+\hat{j}+2 \hat{k})(2 \hat{i}-2 \hat{j}+4 \hat{k})}{\sqrt{9+1+4} \sqrt{4+4+16}}=\frac{12}{\sqrt{336}}=\frac{3}{\sqrt{21}} {/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \theta=\cos ^{-1}\left(\frac{12}{\sqrt{336}}\right) \text { or } \cos ^{-1}\left(\frac{3}{\sqrt{21}}\right) {/tex} 2 OR (ii) Find a vector of magnitude 21 units in the direction opposite to that of {tex}\overrightarrow{A B}{/tex} where {tex}A{/tex} and {tex}B{/tex} are the points {tex}A(2,1,3){/tex} and {tex}B(8,-1,0){/tex} respectively. Solution: {tex}\overrightarrow{B A}=-6 \hat{i}+2 \hat{j}+3 \hat{k}{/tex} Required unit vector of magnitude 21 {tex}=21 \times\left(\frac{-6 \hat{i}+2 \hat{j}+3 \hat{k}}{\sqrt{36+4+9}}\right){/tex} {tex}=3(-6 \hat{i}+2 \hat{j}+3 \hat{k}){/tex} or {tex}-18 \hat{i}+6 \hat{j}+9 \hat{k}{/tex} 2 22. Find the values of 'a' for which {tex}{f}(x)=x^{2}-2 {a} x+{b}{/tex} is an increasing function for {tex}x \gt 0{/tex} . Solution: {tex}f^{\prime}(x)=2 x-2 a{/tex} {tex}0 \lt x \lt \infty \Rightarrow-2 a \lt 2 x-2 a \lt \infty {/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow-2 a \lt f^{\prime}(x) \lt \infty{/tex} {tex}f(x){/tex} is increasing if {tex}f^{\prime}(x) \geq 0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow-2 a \in[0, \infty) \Rightarrow a \in(-\infty, 0]{/tex} or {tex}(-\infty, 0){/tex} 2 23. (i) Differentiate {tex}2^{\cos ^{2} x}{/tex} w.r.t {tex}\cos ^{2} x{/tex} . Solution: Let {tex}u=2^{\cos ^2 x} \Rightarrow \frac{d u}{d x}=2^{\cos ^2 x}(-2 \cos x \sin x) \log 2{/tex} Let {tex}v=\cos ^2 x \Rightarrow \frac{d v}{d x}=-2 \cos x \sin x{/tex} Now {tex}\frac{d u}{d v}=\frac{\left(\frac{d u}{d x}\right)}{\left(\frac{d v}{d x}\right)}=2^{\cos ^2 x} \log 2{/tex} 2 OR (ii) If {tex}\tan ^{-1}\left(x^{2}+y^{2}\right)=a^{2}{/tex} , then find {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}{/tex} . Solution: {tex}\tan ^{-1}\left(x^2+y^2\right)=a^2 \Rightarrow x^2+y^2=\tan a^2{/tex} Differentiate both sides wrt {tex}x{/tex} , {tex}2 x+2 y \frac{d y}{d x}=0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d y}{d x}=-\frac{x}{y}{/tex} 2 24. Evaluate: {tex}\sin ^{-1}\left(\sin \frac{3 \pi}{5}\right){/tex} Solution: {tex}\sin ^{-1}\left(\sin \frac{3 \pi}{5}\right) =\sin ^{-1}\left(\sin \left(\pi-\frac{2 \pi}{5}\right)\right){/tex} {tex}=\sin ^{-1}\left(\sin \left(\frac{2 \pi}{5}\right)\right){/tex} {tex}=\frac{2 \pi}{5}{/tex} 2 25. The diagonals of a parallelogram are given by {tex}\vec{a}=2 \hat{i}-\hat{j}+\hat{k}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{{b}}=\hat{{i}}+3 \hat{{j}}-\hat{{k}}{/tex} . Find the area of the parallelogram.
